---
description: Preview a video before the slow render — a per-scene HTML storyboard showing the exact image/background each scene will use, so the draft is right the first time
---
<!-- AUTO-GENERATED from .claude/commands/storyboard.md by pipeline/sync_harness.py — edit the SOURCE under .claude/, then re-run `python3 pipeline/sync_harness.py sync`. Do not edit this file by hand. -->
Storyboard a TikiTakaFootyTV video **before** rendering it, so the owner can see which image /
background / sticker each scene will get and fix anything wrong while it's still free and fast.
Optional target: $ARGUMENTS

**Why this exists:** the Remotion draft render is the slow step. This runs only the FREE, FAST
half — fetch images + cut out the subject + resolve every scene's visual with the SAME resolver
the render uses (no VO, no render) — and emits an HTML contact sheet. Iterate here for free, then
render the draft once. Bonus: the images it downloads are cached, so the eventual draft is faster.

Run in order:

1. **Resolve the spec.** If `$ARGUMENTS` names a spec (a stem like `australia-turkiye-irankunda-shock`
   or a path), use it. Otherwise use the most recently modified `out/specs/*.json`
   (`ls -t out/specs/*.json | head -1`) and say which one you picked.

2. **(Only if the spec has `commons` scenes)** For an accurate storyboard of one-off `commons`
   images, vision-vet them first — `uv run pipeline/fetch_scene_candidates.py out/specs/<stem>.json`
   then the **`pick-images`** skill — exactly as before a render. Curated-entity / stock_video /
   stock-only specs skip this. (Without it, a `commons` scene shows the raw first search hit, which
   keyword search often gets wrong.)

3. **Build the storyboard.** `bash pipeline/storyboard.sh out/specs/<stem>.json`. This ensures
   assets, runs `prepare.mjs` in storyboard mode (`TTV_STORYBOARD=1`, no VO), and writes
   `out/storyboards/<stem>.html` + `<stem>.summary.json`. It spends **no** ElevenLabs credits and
   runs **no** render.

4. **Report what resolved — then STOP.** Read `out/storyboards/<stem>.summary.json` and tell the owner:
   - the storyboard path: `out/storyboards/<stem>.html` (they open it to eyeball every scene);
   - a one-line-per-scene table: scene # · `visual_source: visual_query` · resolved? (image/video/none) · planned seconds;
   - **every warning**, called out plainly, e.g.:
     - a scene whose source **did not resolve** (renders on a plain brand graphic) → likely a wrong slug/query or an entity needing curation;
     - an **`ai`** scene (AI backgrounds aren't wired → renders with no background; re-route to entity/commons/stock_video);
     - a **`stock_video` freeze risk** (planned ≥ ~6s vs ~6s clip → may freeze on the last frame; shorten the VO or pick a longer clip);
     - a **missing matchup flag** (the scoreboard/VS badge won't render) or a **missing subject sticker**;
   - the exact next steps (do NOT run these yourself):
     - to fix a visual: edit `visual_source` / `visual_query` (or the matchup/entity slug) in
       `out/specs/<stem>.json`, then re-run `/storyboard` (free, seconds) until it looks right;
     - when the visuals are right: render the free draft —
       `bash pipeline/make_video.sh out/specs/<stem>.json`.

Guardrails:
- **No VO, no render, no posting here.** This is a pure preview. Never run `make_video.sh` or set
  `TTV_PRODUCTION=1` from this command.
- Don't declare the visuals "good" — the storyboard is for the **owner** to judge. Surface the
  facts (what resolved, what's risky) and let them decide.
- This slots into `/daily` between `fact-check` and `make_video.sh`: spec → fact-check →
  **/storyboard (review visuals)** → free draft render.
